FluentGpu
Table of Contents

Class TextLayoutEngine

Namespace
FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite
Assembly
FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.dll

The DirectWrite layout engine (text.md §8): itemize → shape (per run) → wrap (UAX #14 break opportunities) → position, with BiDi L2 reordering per line. Drives BOTH measurement (metrics) and rendering (positioned glyphs) so they layout identically — and the editor queries (HitTest(Point2, out bool)/CaretAt(int, out float, out float, out float, out int)/RangeRects(int, int, Span<RectF>)) read the retained per-glyph cluster + per-line tables of the SAME layout, so hit-testing matches rendering exactly. Owns its itemizer + shaper + face cache; reusable, single-thread-confined. Results live in reused grow-only buffers (valid until the next Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char>, string, int, float, float, int, int, int, float, float, int, int, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>, StringTable?); queries fire per keystroke/pointer-move with 0 steady-state allocation). v1: design-advance shaping with kerning/ligatures/complex-script; font fallback + color glyphs are layered on in later phases; query edge mapping is leading=left (LTR — BiDi-correct edges arrive with the RTL workstream, but the scan is already cluster-correct and walks the visual order).

public sealed class TextLayoutEngine : IDisposable
Inheritance
object
TextLayoutEngine

Properties

Baseline 

public float Baseline { get; }

Property Value

float

Clusters

Cluster map of the current layout, in placement (visual) order — parallel to Glyphs minus the synthetic trim ellipsis. Valid until the next Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char>, string, int, float, float, int, int, int, float, float, int, int, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>, StringTable?).

public ReadOnlySpan<LaidCluster> Clusters { get; }

Property Value

ReadOnlySpan<LaidCluster>

Property Value

ReadOnlySpan<LaidGlyph>

Height 

public float Height { get; }

Property Value

float

LineCount 

public int LineCount { get; }

Property Value

int

Lines

Per-line table of the current layout (always ≥ 1 entry, even for empty text). Valid until the next Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char>, string, int, float, float, int, int, int, float, float, int, int, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>, StringTable?).

public ReadOnlySpan<LaidLine> Lines { get; }

Property Value

ReadOnlySpan<LaidLine>

StrikeY

Strikethrough bar position, measured DOWN from the line top (same frame as UnderlineY).

public float StrikeY { get; }

Property Value

float

UnderlineThickness 

public float UnderlineThickness { get; }

Property Value

float

UnderlineY

Underline bar position for the laid family/size, measured DOWN from the line top (top-down DIP; DWrite's positive-up design value is flipped over Baseline). Per-line: add the line's Top.

public float UnderlineY { get; }

Property Value

float

Width 

public float Width { get; }

Property Value

float

Methods

CaretAt(int, out float, out float, out float, out int)

Caret geometry for a UTF-16 index. Returns the LEADING position of charIndex: at a soft-wrap boundary that is the START of the continuation line — trailing affinity (pinning the caret to the END of the wrapped line) is the caller's job; charIndex == text length → the trailing edge of the last line. An index inside a multi-char cluster (ligature/surrogate pair) snaps forward to the cluster's trailing edge. Out-of-range indices clamp.

public void CaretAt(int charIndex, out float x, out float lineTop, out float lineHeight, out int lineIndex)

Parameters

charIndex int
x float
lineTop float
lineHeight float
lineIndex int

Dispose()

Performs application-defined tasks associated with freeing, releasing, or resetting unmanaged resources.

public void Dispose()

HitTest(Point2, out bool)

Point → UTF-16 insertion index. The index is SNAPPED to the nearest edge of the hit glyph cluster — its leading edge when the point falls in the left half, its trailing edge (= the next cluster's start, or the line end) in the right half — and trailing reports which. Out-of-bounds points clamp: above → first line, below → last line, left → line start, right → line end; a hard-broken line clamps to the position BEFORE its terminator cluster, so a click past the text never jumps the caret to the next line. Multi-glyph clusters (base + marks) are walked as one box; an index is never produced inside a cluster.

public int HitTest(Point2 point, out bool trailing)

Parameters

point Point2
trailing bool

Returns

int

Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char>, string, int, float, float, int, int, int, float, float, int, int, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>, StringTable?)

Lay out text; fills Glyphs + metrics. wrap/trim are the TextWrap/TextTrim enum ints; size is DIP. weight is the NUMERIC font weight — the int IS the DWRITE_FONT_WEIGHT (clamped 1..999; ≤0 → 400). charSpacing is WinUI CharacterSpacing in 1/1000 em; lineHeight (DIP; NaN/≤0 = font-natural) resolves per stacking (LineStacking enum int) and lineBounds (TextLineBounds enum int).

<p><code class="paramref">spans</code> (rtb-01 inline runs): per-char-range style overlays applied over the base style —
the text still shapes as ONE flow (one itemize + one wrap pass, like a WinUI paragraph's inline collection);
each span resolves its own face/weight/size for its range. <code class="paramref">names</code> resolves span family ids
(null ⇒ span families fall back to the base family). Line box: the MAX ascent/descent across base + spans —
one uniform line height per paragraph (the per-line LineStackingStrategy=MaxHeight refinement for mixed-size
lines is future work; uniform-size spans, the overwhelming case, are exact).</p>

public void Layout(ReadOnlySpan<char> text, string family, int weight, float size, float maxWidth, int wrap, int trim, int maxLines, float charSpacing = 0, float lineHeight = NaN, int stacking = 0, int lineBounds = 0, ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle> spans = default, StringTable? names = null)

Parameters

text ReadOnlySpan<char>
family string
weight int
size float
maxWidth float
wrap int
trim int
maxLines int
charSpacing float
lineHeight float
stacking int
lineBounds int
spans ReadOnlySpan<SpanStyle>
names StringTable

RangeRects(int, int, Span<RectF>)

One rect per line fragment covered by [start, end): per line, [max(start, lineStart), min(end, lineEnd)) → x-from/x-to via cluster edges. Returns the count written (≤ rects.Length; excess fragments are dropped — callers size generously). Zero-width ranges — and fragments whose clusters have no advance (e.g. a bare line terminator) — produce nothing.

public int RangeRects(int start, int end, Span<RectF> rects)

Parameters

start int
end int
rects Span<RectF>

Returns

int

SelfTest() 

public static void SelfTest()
FluentGpu — a near-zero-allocation, NativeAOT, GPU-rendered UI engine for .NET 10. Usage docs live here; architecture canon lives in design/ (SPEC-INDEX.md is the precedence authority).
